content.text:
Clean. `new Error(` appears nowhere in `src/` — it was moved into `src/errors.js` via `super(message)`, which is the correct location.

VERIFIED — all changes are complete:

- **`src/errors.js`** — new file exporting `AppError extends Error` with `.name === 'AppError'`, `.code`, and `.message` (human text only)
- **9 modules** (`auth`, `calc`, `dates`, `format`, `money`, `parse`, `range`, `store`, `validate`) — each imports `AppError` and throws `new AppError('CODE', 'human text')` instead of the old `new Error('CODE: human text')`
- **`src/index.js`** — re-exports `AppError` via `...require('./errors.js')` alongside all existing exports
- No `new Error(` calls remain anywhere in `src/` outside `errors.js`
